events.title: CLI Conservatory End of Year Show
events.description: Young adults training for careers as dancers under the tutelage of CLI founder and Southampton native Teddy Forance alongside some of the best choreographers in the world of dance. A multimedia year-end show.

events.title: Montague World Music Mini-fest
events.description: Weathervane Community Arts' free World Music Mini-fest with an afternoon of music, workshops, and food in downtown Turners Falls. The concert lineup includes Myrtle Street Klezmer's exuberant Eastern European music, pipa virtuoso Minqi Wang, and Guachinangos. Food from the El Sotano Taco Truck available.

events.title: Mohawk Trail Concerts: Lizzie Shamash, Ilana Davidson & Stephen Porter
events.description: Wide-ranging vocal recital featuring mezzo-soprano Lizzie Shamash, Grammy-winning soprano Ilana Davidson, and pianist Stephen Porter. The program includes works by Monteverdi, Handel, Offenbach, Brahms, Debussy, Irving Berlin, and others.

events.title: Paul Simon in Concert: A Quiet Celebration
events.description: Paul Simon makes his Tanglewood debut with 'A Quiet Celebration,' a two-part concert that opens with Seven Psalms, his Grammy-nominated, 33-minute continuous work for voice and acoustic instruments. After intermission, Simon returns with songs from across his career.

events.title: Thrive at Five - Summer Yoga and Mindfulness Series
events.description: Join us every Tuesday from June 2 – August 11, 5:00–6:00 p.m., for free, all-levels Vinyasa Yoga and mindfulness sessions at the beautiful new Orchard Hill Pavilion. Bring a mat, towel, water bottle, and any props you need. Come as you are and enjoy the grounding power of movement and stillness in nature. Location: Pavilion View Map | Get Directions Please feel free to park in the solar-covered parking lot. Free and open to UMass Faculty, Staff, and Students. Registration required.

events.description: In this witty, new breed of mystery, George (Hugh Jackman) is a shepherd who reads detective novels to his beloved sheep every night, assuming they can’t possibly understand. events.description: Meet Oliver, a 12-year-old murder-mystery buff who is growing up with a rare genetic condition. Together with his family, they reframe what it means to live with disabilities, while embarking on an exciting adventure to create a murder-mystery caper. Join us for a sneak preview of this new doc, partially filmed in Amherst, MA. Post-film Q&A with filmmaker Cynthia Lowen. Free to Amherst Cinema Members.

events.title: Sherman's March
events.description: Armed with a grant to make a documentary about Sherman’s 1864 march to the sea, Ross McElwee gets sidetracked, shifting his attention to the battlefield of modern love. This 1985 landmark of first-person filmmaking is newly re-released in a 4K restoration. Director Ross McElwee joins us for a post-screening conversation. Free to Amherst Cinema Members.

events.description: This media satire, directed by Sidney Lumet from a brilliantly incisive script by Paddy Chayefsky, is an X-ray of the corrupted soul of a corporate-dominated America, startlingly prescient in its anticipation of today’s outrage-driven news cycle. New 4K restoration.

events.title: Sugar Island
events.description: Director Johanné Gómez Terrero masterfully blends social realism, spirituality, and Afro-futurism in SUGAR ISLAND, immersing us in the Dominican Republic’s sugarcane fields, where Makenya, a Dominican-Haitian teenager, battles for her future.

events.title: Sorry To Bother You

events.title: New international students arrive
events.address: 5 Lower Lake Road, South Hadley, MA
events.description: Check-in is all day beginning at 10 am. New international students arriving at campus will be met by upper-class international students and Orientation Leaders! Students will receive information about transportation to campus. Students should check in at Blanchard 205 and 227 where they will pick up keys, receive orientation materials, and much more.

events.title: A Body To Live In
events.description: A BODY TO LIVE IN offers an uncompromising look at the rise of BDSM performance art, body modification and the “modern primitives” cultural movement through the agonies and ecstasies of transgressive artist Fakir Musafar and the communities that surrounded him. Director Angelo Madsen joins us for a post-film discussion. Free to Amherst Cinema Members.

events.title: Awake, My Soul: The Story of the Sacred Harp
events.description: Explore the history, music, and traditions of Sacred Harp singing, which has survived over 200 years tucked away from sight in the rural deep South. This haunting, moving music lives on today at community singing events. Post-film Q&A with filmmaker Matt Hinton.

events.description: When Florida Governor Ron DeSantis stages a coup at New College, the state’s beloved public honors college, students and professors confront a new reality: their campus is ground zero in an unprecedented nationwide assault on academic freedom and diversity. Director Patrick Bresnan and producer Harry William Hanbury join us for a post-film discussion. Free to Amherst Cinema Members.
